cit.basis: No general corporate income tax in Bahrain (zero-CIT model); only oil and gas exploration/production is taxed.
cit.note: Oil & gas taxed at 46% under Legislative Decree No. 22 of 1979. From 1 January 2025 Bahrain applies a 15% Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax (DMTT, Decree-Law No. 11 of 2024) to large MNEs with consolidated revenue >= EUR 750m — the first GCC state to do so; the DMTT targets large multinationals and is out of scope for this SME estimator.

vat.note: VAT standard rate 10%, raised from 5% on 1 January 2022; administered by the National Bureau for Revenue (NBR).

personal_income_tax.note: No personal income tax on employment income.

social_insurance.note: Social Insurance Organization (SIO): Bahraini employees 7% employee / 12% employer (including 1% unemployment insurance); expatriates 1% employee / 3% employer.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
DMTT (large MNEs), sector-specific rules, relief, and filing rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
